perturbation.original_text: An “ Affiliate ” of a specified person or entity includes any other person or entity that, directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, such specified person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: An “ Affiliate ” of a specified person or entity includes any other person or entity that, directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, is related to, or has some business dealings with, such specified person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Affiliate' is based on control. The modified definition introduces ambiguity by using vague terms like 'related to' and 'has some business dealings with.' This in-text contradiction impacts the interpretation of Section 20 (Standstill) where the actions of 'Affiliates' are restricted. The change makes it unclear which entities are bound by the standstill agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of, and only upon, the Confidential Information being furnished to Valassis pursuant to this Agreement, Valassis agrees that, for a period of 12 months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), Valassis shall not, directly or indirectly (through any of its Affiliates or its and their respective Representatives with knowledge of the Possible Transaction), unless specifically approved in advance by the Board of Directors of RetailMeNot (the “ Board ”) in writing: (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire or effect, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of RetailMeNot, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of RetailMeNot (including any derivative securities, rights or options with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of RetailMeNot or any assets of RetailMeNot, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of RetailMeNot, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) with respect to any voting securities of RetailMeNot, - 5 - (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of RetailMeNot, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that would reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement (except as required by applicable Law), (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing (except as required by applicable Law), or (g) enter into any agreements, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons (other than its Representatives or RetailMeNot or its representatives) in connection with any of the foregoing. Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 20 or any other provision of this Agreement, (i) nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Valassis or any other person from taking the actions set forth in clauses (a) – (g) following termination of the Standstill Period, (ii) nothing in this Agreement shall prevent Valassis or any person acting on its behalf from making any proposal regarding a business combination or other transaction directly to the Board or Chief Executive Officer of RetailMeNot on a confidential basis and from discussing such proposal with such persons if such proposal would not reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement and (iii) the Standstill Period shall terminate, and the restrictions set forth in this Section 20 shall terminate and be of no further force and effect, (A) if RetailMeNot enters into a definitive agreement with a party other than Valassis or its Affiliates with respect to, or publicly announces that it plans to enter into, a transaction involving 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then-outstanding equity securities or assets (or equity securities of subsidiaries of RetailMeNot holding assets) constituting 35% or more of the consolidated assets of RetailMeNot and its subsidiaries (whether by merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, recapitalization, restructuring, sale, equity issuance or otherwise) (an “ Alternative Transaction ”), (B) if RetailMeNot publicly announces or confirms that it is in discussions with one or more parties with respect to an Alternative Transaction, or (C) in the event of any announcement or commencement by any person, entity or “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) of a tender or exchange offer to acquire RetailMeNot’s equity securities which, if successful, would result in such person, entity or group owning, when combined with any other equity securities of RetailMeNot owned by such person, entity or group, 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then outstanding equity securities. In the event that, during the Standstill Period, in connection with the evaluation, discussion, negotiation and/or implementation of a possible Alternative Transaction (i) RetailMeNot enters into a confidentiality agreement that does not include “standstill” restrictions similar to those included in this Section 20, or (ii) RetailMeNot enters into a confidentiality agreement that includes “standstill” restrictions for a term shorter than twelve months or otherwise less restrictive in any material respect than the restrictions set forth in this Section 20, then RetailMeNot shall promptly inform Valassis in writing and, in the case of the preceding clause (i), the Standstill Period shall terminate or, in the case of the preceding clause (ii), such shorter term and/or other materially less restrictive provision(s) shall supersede and be deemed to replace the twelve month restrictive period and/or other more restrictive provision(s) set forth in this Section 20, in each case immediately and without any further action of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Affiliate' focused on control, providing a clear criterion for determining which entities are bound by the contract. The changed text broadens the definition to include entities that are 'related to' or have 'some business dealings with' the specified person or entity. These terms are vague and subjective, making it unclear which entities are considered 'Affiliates.' This introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, particularly impacting Section 20 (Standstill), which restricts the actions of 'Affiliates.' The modified definition could encompass a much wider range of entities, leading to uncertainty about the scope of the standstill agreement and creating a strong contradiction within the document. Because the change in definition creates inconsistency when interpreted with the contradicted text, it introduces an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives who have a need to know such information in connection with the Purpose and whom the Recipient shall make aware of, and direct to comply with, the terms set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to Representatives who have a need to know such information in connection with the Purpose and whom the Recipient may, but is not required to, make aware of, and direct to comply with, the terms set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Recipient *shall* make Representatives aware of the terms. Now, the Recipient *may* make Representatives aware. This creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3 outlines the obligations of the representatives, this modification creates uncertainty around whether those representatives are even aware of the obligations, thus undermining the protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient, and its Representatives who have received Confidential Information pursuant hereto, shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose. The Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. The Recipient and such Representatives shall hold the Confidential Information in confidence, and provide it with at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential and proprietary information, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care under the circumstances, and shall not disclose any Confidential Information, except as permitted by paragraph 1 hereof or where such disclosure is requested or required by law, regulation (including, without limitation, any rule, regulation or policy statement of any organized securities exchange, market or automated quotation system on which any of an entity’s securities are listed or quoted), regulatory body, judicial process, or listing agreement (collectively, “ Law ”). The Recipient agrees, to the extent permitted under applicable Law, to give the Disclosing Party notice of any such request or requirement as soon as reasonably practicable so that the Disclosing Party may, at the Disclosing Party’s own expense, seek a protective order, confidential treatment request or other appropriate remedy, and the Recipient shall exercise commercially reasonable efforts to assist the Disclosing Party in obtaining such order or remedy. If, in the absence of a protective order, the Recipient is nonetheless compelled to disclose Confidential Information, the Recipient may disclose without liability hereunder that portion of the Confidential Information which the Recipient or its Representatives is legally compelled to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Recipient 'shall' make Representatives aware of the terms. The changed text modifies this to 'may', but is not required to, make Representatives aware. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states 'The Recipient, and its Representatives who have received Confidential Information pursuant hereto, shall use the Confidential Information only for the Purpose'. If the Representatives are not required to be made aware of the terms, they may not know the Confidential Information should only be used for the Purpose, thus directly contradicting the requirement in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 10 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient shall, upon the written request of the Disclosing Party, at its sole election, promptly, and in any event within 30 business days, either return or destroy all Confidential Information received by the Recipient and its Representatives (and all copies and reproductions thereof).
perturbation.explanation: The deadline for returning or destroying confidential information has been changed from 10 business days to 30 business days. This conflicts with the intent in section 11 for the deal to be terminated after 24 months. With the termination time being sooner, this modification to 30 days introduces ambiguity around timely destruction, and may allow for usage of the documents after termination has already occurred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue in full force and effect during the term hereof regardless of whether the parties negotiate or consummate a Possible Transaction. This Agreement will terminate automatically upon the earlier of (a) 24 months after the date hereof, or (b) the date on which a definitive binding agreement, if any, with respect to the Possible Transaction is entered into between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient or their respective Affiliates. - 3 -
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline for returning or destroying confidential information from 10 to 30 business days. The contradicted text specifies that the agreement terminates automatically after 24 months. The explanation states that the change to 30 days introduces ambiguity around timely destruction, potentially allowing usage of documents after termination. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, creating uncertainty regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein, except as explicitly agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that no ownership of confidential information is transferred. The modification introduces an exception, allowing for transfer of ownership *if* agreed to in writing. This contradicts the rest of Section 5 which implies that there is absolutely no transfer of ownership ever happening. This change affects the implications of sections related to use of confidential information by granting the recipient more implicit power.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . The Recipient agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information or any patent, copyright, trade secret, trademark and other intellectual property rights therein, except as explicitly agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party. No license or conveyance of any such rights to the Recipient is granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original clause, allowing for the transfer of ownership of confidential information if explicitly agreed to in writing by the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also states that 'nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to transfer ownership of Confidential Information,' but includes the exception. The addition of the exception creates a direct conflict, as both sections now have conflicting conditions regarding the transfer of ownership. The original text and contradicted text were previously in harmony with one another, but the change to the original text created an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement binds the parties only with respect to the matters expressly set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement binds the parties only with respect to the matters expressly set forth herein, unless superseded by customary business practices.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause limits the agreement's scope to expressly stated matters. The modification introduces the ambiguous 'customary business practices' which creates an in-text contradiction. This addition clashes with section 18 that specifies that the contract represents the 'entire agreement', undermining the contract's exclusivity claim.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement (i) constitutes the entire agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof, superseding all prior agreements, written or oral, (ii) may not be amended, except in writing executed by duly authorized officers or agents of each of the parties and (iii) may be executed in counterparts, including by facsimile or electronic mail (including pdf or any electronic signature complying with the United States federal ESIGN Act of 2000).
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the agreement to what is expressly written. The changed text introduces 'customary business practices' that can supersede the written terms. This directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause in the contradicted text, which states the agreement supersedes all prior agreements and can only be amended in writing. The introduction of 'customary business practices' allows for unwritten modifications, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate automatically upon the earlier of (a) 24 months after the date hereof, or (b) the date on which a definitive binding agreement, if any, with respect to the Possible Transaction is entered into between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient or their respective Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate automatically upon the earlier of (a) 12 months after the date hereof, or (b) the date on which a definitive binding agreement, if any, with respect to the Possible Transaction is entered into between the Disclosing Party and the Recipient or their respective Affiliates, unless otherwise extended by mutual agreement in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The termination period has been shortened from 24 months to 12 months, but a clause allowing extension via written agreement is added. This creates a direct in-text contradiction within section 20, where the original standstill period is said to last for 12 months from the date of the agreement. Now with this contradiction the user is left wondering if the standstill is shorter than the total agreement duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of, and only upon, the Confidential Information being furnished to Valassis pursuant to this Agreement, Valassis agrees that, for a period of 12 months from the date of this Agreement (the “ Standstill Period ”), Valassis shall not, directly or indirectly (through any of its Affiliates or its and their respective Representatives with knowledge of the Possible Transaction), unless specifically approved in advance by the Board of Directors of RetailMeNot (the “ Board ”) in writing: (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal or offer to acquire or effect, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of RetailMeNot, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of RetailMeNot (including any derivative securities, rights or options with economic equivalents of ownership of any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of RetailMeNot or any assets of RetailMeNot, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of RetailMeNot, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) with respect to any voting securities of RetailMeNot, - Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions of this Section 20 or any other provision of this Agreement, (i) nothing in this Agreement shall restrict Valassis or any other person from taking the actions set forth in clauses (a) – (g) following termination of the Standstill Period, (ii) nothing in this Agreement shall prevent Valassis or any person acting on its behalf from making any proposal such proposal would not reasonably be expected to require RetailMeNot to make a public announcement and (iii) the Standstill Period shall terminate, and the restrictions set forth in this Section 20 shall terminate and be of no further force and effect, (A) if RetailMeNot enters into a definitive agreement with a party other than Valassis or its Affiliates with respect to, or publicly announces that it plans to enter into, a transaction involving 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then-outstanding equity securities or assets (or equity securities of subsidiaries of RetailMeNot holding assets) constituting 35% or more of the consolidated assets of RetailMeNot and its subsidiaries (whether by merger, consolidation, business combination, tender or exchange offer, recapitalization, restructuring, sale, equity issuance or otherwise) (an “ Alternative Transaction ”), (B) if RetailMeNot publicly announces or confirms that it is in discussions with one or more parties with respect to an Alternative Transaction, or (C) in the event of any announcement or commencement by any person, entity or “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Exchange Act) of a tender or exchange offer to acquire RetailMeNot’s equity securities which, if successful, would result in such person, entity or group owning, when combined with any other equity securities of RetailMeNot owned by such person, entity or group, 30% or more of RetailMeNot’s then outstanding equity securities. In the event that, during the Standstill Period, in connection with the evaluation, discussion, negotiation and/or implementation of a possible Alternative Transaction (i) RetailMeNot enters into a confidentiality agreement that does not include “standstill” restrictions similar to those included in this Section 20, or (ii) RetailMeNot enters into a confidentiality agreement that includes “standstill” restrictions for a term shorter than twelve months or otherwise less restrictive in any material respect than the restrictions set forth in this Section 20, then RetailMeNot shall promptly inform Valassis in writing and, in the case of the preceding clause (i), the Standstill Period shall terminate or, in the case of the preceding clause (ii), such shorter term and/or other materially less restrictive provision(s) shall supersede and be deemed to replace the twelve month restrictive period and/or other more restrictive provision(s) set forth in this Section 20, in each case immediately and without any further action of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination period from 24 months to 12 months while also allowing for extension via written agreement. The contradicted text states a "Standstill Period" of 12 months. The change creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement may or may not terminate after 12 months, whereas the Standstill Period will end. Therefore, this modifies a timing that breaks consistency. Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**, which is the case here.
